title: trollius europaeus subsp. transsilvanicus
intro: Trollius europaeus subsp. transsilvanicus, also known as the Transylvanian globe-flower, is a subspecies of the globe-flower plant, which belongs to the buttercup family and is native to the Carpathian Mountains in Romania. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Trollius europaeus subsp. transsilvanicus include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Trollius europaeus subsp. transsilvanicus is typically used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
